title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mrs. Bantry and Miss Marple discuss the sudden death of Heather Badcock, which occurred the previous day. Mrs. Bantry explains that Heather arrived at the event full of energy but collapsed and died only fifteen minutes later. Mrs. Bantry learned of the incident through gossip, specifically from Old Briggs, who overheard Dr. Sutherford calling the police station while tending his garden.

Miss Marple rejects the possibility that Heather committed suicide, noting that Heather was a kind person. She compares Heather to a woman named Alison Wilder, describing a specific type of self-centeredness where an individual is unselfish but lacks a sense of self-preservation or awareness of how their actions affect others. Miss Marple suggests that Heather may have been murdered because she was unaware of a danger or perhaps due to mistaken identity, though she clarifies that Heather was not the type of person to engage in extortion.

Dr. Haydock visits Miss Marple and mentions that rumors in St. Mary Mead already suggest the death is a murder case, even before the autopsy is performed. He notes that the autopsy is scheduled for the following day. After the doctor leaves, Mrs. Bantry reveals she was present at the house when the death occurred. She explains that a slender young man, possibly Marina Gregg's secretary, brought her into the house to a reception area set up on a landing where a dressing room and bedroom had been removed to create an alcove.
